_doc: Surface map for refresh_perf_citations.py. Each entry describes ONE citation in ONE file. The script tries to find `find` (regex), and if matched, replaces with `replace` (template using {key} from canonical_numbers.json). Type 'manual' entries are listed in the output checklist for human edit (used when a regex/single-line replace is too risky — e.g., table restructures, FAQ rewrites).

_replacement_types.regex: Standard find-and-replace. `find` is a regex, `replace` is a Python format-string with {canonical_key} placeholders.
_replacement_types.block: Replace everything between two literal start/end markers (e.g., `{/* SURFACE-MARKER:foo-START */}` and `{/* SURFACE-MARKER:foo-END */}`) with `replace_template`. The markers themselves stay; the content between them is fully replaced. Idempotent.
_replacement_types.manual: Cannot be safely auto-replaced. Listed in the manual checklist with line hints + guidance.

entries.description: FAQ 'What returns should I actually expect?' — currently cites simulation 23% + friction-adjusted 21.5% framing
entries.current: 'The simulation average is ~23% annualized. After estimating realistic slippage and commissions, that figure drops to roughly 21.5% — still meaningfully above...'
entries.target: Drop simulation/friction split; use walk-forward result directly: 'The 5-year walk-forward average is ~{wf_5y_avg_annualized_pct_marketing}% annualized — meaningfully above the S&P's ~13% historical, with worst-case start date still returning +{wf_5y_worst_start_pct_rounded}% over five years. Past performance does not predict future results.'
entries.guidance: Rewrite as walk-forward-direct framing. No friction haircut narrative needed (most readers don't notice it).

entries.description: FAQ 'Why publish the lower number?' — this question becomes incoherent when there's no two-column framing
entries.target: Either delete this FAQ entry, or pivot to: 'Why don't you publish higher numbers like other services?' with answer about avoiding cherry-picked windows / over-fit backtests / outlier-inflated averages.
entries.guidance: Recommend deletion; the question doesn't make sense once the friction-adjusted column is gone.

out_of_repo_checklist.what_to_check: Any cited annualized return, avg return, or 'beat the market by X%' language
Same as Twitter — bio + pinned post
If product description cites returns, update via Stripe dashboard
Pull DB social_posts WHERE status='scheduled' OR status='approved' AND scheduled_for > NOW() — review for stale numbers
After investor-report-html edits, regenerate the PDF and re-upload to wherever it's distributed
